check.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "$p=Join-Path $env:ProgramFiles 'Apache24\conf\httpd.conf'; $module=Select-String -Path $p -Pattern '^\s*LoadModule\s+reqtimeout_module\b.*mod_reqtimeout' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 1; $directive=Select-String -Path $p -Pattern '^\s*RequestReadTimeout\b\s+\S+' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 1; if ($module -and $directive) { 'Compliant' }"

candidate_check.description: The Apache web server must set an inactive timeout for completing the TLS handshake

check_content_excerpt:
Review the <'INSTALL PATH'>\conf\httpd.conf file.

Verify the "mod_reqtimeout" is loaded.

If it does not exist, this is a finding.

If the "mod_reqtimeout" module is loaded and the "RequestReadTimeout" directive is not configured, this is a finding.

Note: The "RequestReadTimeout" directive must be explicitly configured (i.e., not left to a default value) to a value compatible with the organization's operations.
fix_text_excerpt:
Edit the <'INSTALL PATH'>\conf\httpd.conf file and load the "mod_reqtimeout" module.

Set the "RequestReadTimeout" directive to a value compatible with the organization's operations.

Restart the Apache service.
title: The Apache web server must set an inactive timeout for completing the TLS handshake
